content: Alice MCP server started

Bill wants to mine wc2 for features and approaches to harvest into wc3. What do you know about the wc2→wc3 gap analysis? Which wc2 features are missing from wc3? What's the current state of GAP-01 through GAP-15?

**Alice found 5 relevant sources:**

1. **wc3_readmes/junkdrawer/2026-07-01.txt** (distance: 0.3873)
" 2>&1 | tail -5" completed (exit code 0)


WC2 → WC3 Gap Assessment

  GAP Status — What's Done, What's Left

  GAP: GAP-01
  Feature: Order Production
  Status: DONE
  What was built this session: order_production.py rewired to adjust_item_quantity
  What remains: WO spawn needs testing
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  GAP: GAP-02
  Feature: Backorder Management
  Status: DON

[OBSERVE pattern:item] WC2 mining identified 38+ functional clusters not yet in WC3. Highest priority for Alice's domain: pricing/discount engine (multi-level price hierarchy, margin-floor enforcement), commission/rep management (rate tables by margin band), returns/voids/credit memo automation, serial number lifecycle tracking, catalog delta/cross-reference management, demand forecasting (reorder points, 5-year trends), and the B2B exchange protocol for vendor item availability. The cLine class shows the authoritative line calculation: discountedPrice = unitPrice × (1 - discount/100), extendedPrice = qty × discountedPrice. All line prices should be snapshotted at entry time, not derived from current product price.
{"id": 1, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE observe:invoice] WC2 invoice workflow patterns to harvest: (1) Document promotion chain — proposal→order→invoice with one-click Make Order/Make Invoice actions. (2) Per-line tax snapshot at entry time, not derived from current product. (3) Cascade rollup — post_save signal on InvoiceLine triggers invoice.recalculate(). (4) Invoice type enum replacing boolean flags (invoice/proforma/credit_note/quote). (5) Partial payments with Amount vs Applied fields. (6) Print views — browser-native PDF for invoices/orders/proposals. (7) Linked invoice sets for complex billing. (8) Cloneable proposals — search past proposals, one-click clone to current customer. (9) AR statement generation with aging buckets. (10) Period close — day open/close discipline gating month-end operations.
{"id": 2, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE observe:customer] WC2 customer/CRM patterns to harvest: (1) Employee Home dashboard — single-page workbench with shared date-range filter driving all sub-lists (customers, orders, proposals, invoices, services). (2) Unified search hub — 8 parallel search widgets on one page. (3) CustomerSelection date-range query methods (actionToday, actionDate, actionDateRange) as Django QuerySet managers. (4) cAddress locale-aware formatting (US/France/international). (5) cPerson name parsing with qqname_o structure (prefix/first/last/middle/nick/attention/phones[]/emails[]). (6) Call report form — structured CRM activity log with ad source, contact method, action. (7) Daily lead processing batch. (8) Customer statement generation with aging.
{"id": 3, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE pattern:action] 4D EA_Tasks pattern for WC3 Action model: Status↔Percent coupling as state machine. Completed→100%, Not Started→0%, In Progress→50% default. Compound filter pattern: text + category + percent range composed additively. Validation chain accumulates ALL errors before showing (field-keyed error dict). Toggle-all enable/disable for read-only vs edit mode. Priority as radio→integer mapping (0=None, 1=Low, 2=High). Delete navigates to adjacent record. Auto-delete orphaned categories.
{"id": 4, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE pattern:invoice] 4D EA_Invoices and UI-with-Classes patterns for WC3 DataBrowser: (1) Per-user per-table column layout persistence (Listbox_Setting table). (2) Generic property introspection for search/sort — walk model fields, handle FK/object sub-paths, exclude _prefixed fields. (3) Operator vocabulary as data — {text, operator} objects mapped to Django ORM lookups (__startswith, __icontains, __gt, __lt). (4) Type-aware query routing — numeric→range, text→string ops, date→picker, FK→typeahead. (5) Faceted sidebar — group by distinct field value + count. (6) Computed properties on Entity subclasses (Amount_Due, Full_Address, Sales_Amount) as @property on Django models. (7) Cascade delete in transaction (lines before header). (8) __DeletedRecords soft-delete audit table. (9) Pessimistic lock UI showing WHO has the lock. (10) Optimistic lock with entity resurrection if deleted between load and save.
{"id": 5, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE observe:contact] 4D EA_Contacts patterns for WC3: (1) Dual display mode — list view vs card grid toggle with responsive column calculation. (2) Live keystroke search with composited query template (query shape separated from search value). (3) Save creates FK category on-the-fly if new, looks up if existing, blanks if empty — three-way branch. (4) Inline FK autocomplete with dynamic-sized dropdown, limit 10. (5) Delete navigates to next/prev record (doSafeSelect). (6) Controller-as-message-bus — named string events dispatched through Case-of. (7) BOM/recipe pattern — parent + line items with qty+unit, thumbnail vs full image, structured blob for ordered sub-items, faceted sidebar navigation.
{"id": 6, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE pattern:action] Bill confirmed DataBrowser JSON-schema-defines-forms as the r25 foundation. Key extensions for layout marketplace: widget type schema catalog, separate list/detail layouts, dataSource+typeHint pair, fixed/grow sizing in config, save/restore layout snapshots, per-user per-model persistence. Layout marketplace: users submit layouts for credit, Alice tracks adoption rates per layout to surface the best ones.
{"id": 7, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE pattern:invoice] WC3 gap analysis: Invoice model missing invoice_type column (invoice/proforma/credit_note). Currently buried in refs.source JSONB — not queryable. Per-type numbering sequences not implemented. Credit notes have no guard against being paid or double-counted in AR. AuditLog model exists but nothing auto-writes to it — no post_save signal captures field-level diffs. Version conflict checking is disabled in save_view.py — concurrent edits silently overwrite. Tax rate stored in dual keys (cost.tax_rate AND tax.sales_rate) which can drift apart.
{"id": 8, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE pattern:payment] WC3 gap: Stripe webhook deduplication uses gateway_payment_intent_id lookup but no DB unique constraint — two simultaneous webhooks for same intent can both create Payment records. PaymentApplication.clean() validates over-application but clean() is not called on save() by default in Django — guard may not fire in API save paths. Need: unique constraint on gateway_payment_intent_id, call full_clean() in save or pre_save signal.
{"id": 9, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE observe:item] WC3 gap: Connection model schema is well-defined but execution layer absent — no upload()/download()/list() methods. Each integration (email verification, Google Calendar, address verification) is a standalone service not wired through Connection. SFTP type exists in choices but no executor. DynamicCatalogs integration blocked until Connection has an execution protocol. Import wizard is CLI-only (import_items_tsv.py) — no HTTP upload→preview→map→execute flow for operators.
{"id": 10, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE observe:item] WC3 model inventory: 95 models across 10 apps. Key structural finding: products app has 22 models including BillOfMaterial, Serial, SerialLog, Warehouse, InventoryLayer, SiteInventory, InventoryMovement — many WC2 features already have model stubs. Transactions has Requisition+RequisitionLine (pre-PO approval gate that WC2 had). The gap is not models existing — it's models being wired to workflows and UI.
{"id": 12, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE pattern:item] WC3 model gap analysis: The real gap is workflows not models. 95 models exist including BOM, Serial, Warehouse, InventoryLayer, Requisition, Catalog, Delivery, GL, TaxJurisdiction — all stubs awaiting wiring. Only 10 genuinely new models needed: PriceLevel, PriceMatrix, CommissionRule, CommissionEntry, PackingList, PackingLine, LoadTag, EDIMessage, EDIMap, EmailTemplate, EmailQueue, EmailOptOut, Statement, StatementLine, PeriodClose, LayoutSubmission, SavedSearch. Priority: wire existing models to manage actions and DataBrowser views before building new models.
{"id": 13, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE observe:action] DataBrowser improvements implemented 2026-07-03: (1) doSafeSelect after delete — adjacent record auto-selected. (2) Reset Layout button — loads 'initial' or 'alice_guess' view. (3) Operator vocabulary consolidated — ALLOWED_LOOKUPS in filter_operators.py, filterOperators.ts frontend. (4) Widget type schema — widgetTypes.ts with all 15 types, defaults, sizing, capabilities. (5) typeHint override on FieldSpec — layout can override auto-detected behavior type. (6) Client-side validation — validateRecord.ts accumulates all errors before save. (7) CSS module created — AdminWorkbench.css with custom properties for both themes. CSS migration is incremental.
{"id": 14, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE pattern:action] Field widget architecture deployed 2026-07-04. Alice's role: the field_access Setting record per model IS the instruction set for React rendering. When Alice updates field_behaviors in a Setting (type, required, options, model, display), she directly controls which widget renders and how it behaves. Alice should monitor: (1) models with missing or incomplete field_behaviors, (2) fields where the auto-detected type is wrong (e.g., an IntegerField that should display as boolean), (3) fields where typeHint in the layout overrides the behavior — these may indicate the behavior itself should be updated.
{"id": 15, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE pattern:item] WC2 BOM patterns mined 2026-07-04. WC3 BillOfMaterial model exists with cycle detection, effective dates, scrap factor, revision, cost_snapshot — all solid. Missing operations: (1) multi-level tree expansion (BFS flat list with level tracking), (2) recursive cost rollup propagating sub-assembly costs, (3) three-cost candidates (avg/last/matrix) with forecast option for estimating, (4) consume/produce inventory transaction posting (parent +qty, children -qty, tagged with common adjID), (5) where-used top-level query for impact analysis, (6) on-hand adjusted build qty calculation, (7) BOM export endpoint. The three-cost system is critical for Alice: when a component price changes, Alice should propagate costs up through all ancestor assemblies and flag significant variances.
{"id": 16, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE observe:item] BOM services fully built 2026-07-04. Five new operations added to bom_services.py: (1) expand_tree — BFS multi-level expansion with level/qty/cost tracking, (2) propagate_cost_up — walks up ancestor chain recalculating each parent, (3) consume_bom — atomic inventory movements (+parent, -children) with batch_id tracing, (4) find_top_level_assemblies — where-used impact analysis, (5) calc_net_build_qty — net against on-hand. Four new API endpoints: /items/{id}/bom/expand/, /items/{id}/bom/consume/, /items/{id}/bom/where-used/, /items/{id}/bom/propagate-cost/. Alice should monitor: cost propagation events (watch for large variances), consume operations (watch for negative inventory), where-used queries (signal that a component may be changing).
{"id": 17, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE observe:item] DataGrid tree support built 2026-07-04. Three new props: treeColumn (which column gets indent+chevron), levelField (data field with depth number, default 'level'), childFlag (data field indicating has-children, default 'is_subassembly'). Expand/collapse is client-side filtering of the flat array — collapsed parent hides all deeper rows until same/shallower level appears. Same columns render at every level (recursive by data). Indentation is paddingLeft = level * 20px. Chevron: ▶ collapsed, ▼ expanded, · leaf. Usage for BOM: pass expand_tree API response as records, set treeColumn='item_ida'. No new component needed — DataGrid handles it with the same column definitions at every depth.
{"id": 18, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:item] BOM Training Module for Alice Quiz Engine. Topic: Bill of Materials operations. Questions Alice should ask Bill:

Q1 (beginner): What does the expand_tree endpoint return for a multi-level BOM?
A) A nested JSON tree with children inside parents
B) A flat list with a level field indicating depth
C) Only the direct children of the parent
D) A graphical tree image
Correct: B — expand_tree uses BFS to flatten the hierarchy into a list where each row carries its level number. Same columns at every depth.

Q2 (beginner): What happens when you POST to /bom/consume/ with qty=5 for BB200?
A) Deletes 5 BB200 items from inventory
B) Creates +5 receipt for BB200 and negative issues for each component (qty × 5)
C) Sends a purchase order to the vendor
D) Recalculates the cost of BB200
Correct: B — consume_bom posts a positive inventory movement for the finished assembly and negative movements for each component multiplied by the build quantity.

Q3 (intermediate): BB200 has BB404 x3 as a component. BB404 has BBF x3. If you build 2 BB200 kits, how many BBF are consumed?
A) 3
B) 6
C) 9
D) 18
Correct: D — 2 kits × 3 BB404 per kit × 3 BBF per BB404 = 18 BBF total.

Q4 (intermediate): What does propagate_cost_up do that recalc_parent_cost does not?
A) It calculates tax
B) It walks UP through all ancestor assemblies and recalculates each one
C) It deletes old cost records
D) It exports costs to accounting
Correct: B — recalc_parent_cost only does one level. propagate_cost_up walks the entire ancestor chain so a component cost change flows all the way to the top-level assembly.

Q5 (intermediate): What is the where-used endpoint for?
A) Finding which warehouse an item is stored in
B) Finding all top-level assemblies that contain a given component
C) Showing who last edited the BOM
D) Listing all orders that include the item
Correct: B — find_top_level_assemblies walks UP the BOM from a child to find all root assemblies. Used for impact analysis when a component ch
{"id": 19, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE pattern:action] DataBrowser spawn pattern for complex records: desktop spawns independent windows (users need large/multiple screens), mobile uses tabs. spawn_links in field_access Setting per model: {label, target_model, filter_field, filter_source}. Serial example: [Log] [Q&A] [Documents] [Actions] [Customer] [Vendor] — each opens a DBR filtered via refs.links. Same config drives both viewport modes. Alice should configure spawn_links for: Serial, Item (when has BOM), Invoice (related payments/lines), Contact (related orders/invoices), WorkOrder (related lines/actions).
{"id": 20, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:item] Serial services built 2026-07-04. Alice training for serial quiz: Status lifecycle is available→issued→returned (or available→referenced for customer-owned). Warranty calculated at ISSUE time not receive time (today + warranty_days). warranty_due(30) finds serials expiring within 30 days — Alice should monitor this for proactive service order generation. search_by_customer finds all serials currently with a customer. Every state change logs to SerialLog with action + timestamp + config JSON. The spawn_links pattern applies: Serial detail should spawn Log, Q&A, Documents, Actions, Customer, Vendor windows. refs.links carries all foreign relationships (vendor_id, po_id, customer_id, invoice_id).
{"id": 21, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:action] Alice Training Module — Session 2026-07-03/04. Train Bill on these topics tomorrow:

TOPIC 1: DataBrowser Improvements
- doSafeSelect: after delete, adjacent record auto-selected (no blank pane)
- Reset Layout: button restores initial/alice_guess view
- Operator vocabulary: single source of truth in filterOperators.ts + filter_operators.py
- Widget type schema: widgetTypes.ts defines all 16 types with defaults
- typeHint: layout can override auto-detected field type
- Validation: accumulates ALL errors before showing (validateRecord.ts)
- CSS migration: zero inline styles in AdminWorkbench, all via custom properties

TOPIC 2: Field Widget Suite
- 16 standalone components in components/fields/
- Direct use: <CurrencyField name="total" value={x} onChange={fn} />
- Dynamic use: getWidget('currency') returns the component
- renderField() is what DataBrowser calls
- The field_access Setting IS the instruction set — Alice controls rendering by updating it

TOPIC 3: BOM Operations (use BB200 as live example)
- expand_tree: flat list with levels (test: GET /products/items/244/bom/expand/)
- Multi-level: BB200→BB404→BBF/BBK/BBPV visible in output
- Cost rollup: recalc_parent_cost computes $89.52 from components
- Where-used: BB005 is used in BB200
- Consume: would post +1 BB200, -3 BB005, -2 BB100, etc to inventory
- DataGrid tree mode: treeColumn prop shows indent + chevrons

TOPIC 4: Serial Number Lifecycle
- receive_serial: PO receive → Available
- issue_to_sale: → Issued, warranty starts (today + warranty_days)
- return_from_sale: → Available, customer cleared
- reference_existing: customer-owned serial attached without formal receive
- warranty_due(30): finds serials expiring within 30 days
- search_by_customer/vendor: find who has what
- Every operation logs to SerialLog

TOPIC 5: DataBrowser Spawn Pattern
- Complex records need multiple simultaneous views
- Desktop: spawn independent windows across monitors
- Mobile: tabs within one view
- spa
{"id": 22, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE observe:action] JSON Viewer built 2026-07-04. Standalone window at /json-viewer, spawned from DataBrowser. Zero npm dependencies — custom recursive React component (~200 lines). Features: collapsible tree with depth control, URLs render as clickable links (documents are URL pointers that open directly), _id fields link back to DataBrowser (customer_id opens contact), epoch ms auto-formatted as dates, copy-to-clipboard, dark/light theme via same CSS custom properties. JsonField widget now shows summary ({N keys} or [N items]) with click to spawn viewer. Accepts data via URL params (small JSON) or postMessage (large JSON) or fetches from wcapi by model+id+field.
{"id": 23, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE pattern:item] Bill defined inventory costing rules 2026-07-04. InventoryLayer stacking: each receipt/WO creates a layer at a cost. Four methods: Average (weighted across stack), Last (newest layer), FIFO (ship oldest first), LIFO (ship newest first). Margin velocity is the key metric Alice should track: margin × turns ÷ carry cost. Example: 5% margin × 100 turns/day = better than 100% margin × 1 turn/5 years. Alice should compute margin velocity per item and flag items with high margin but low turns (dead capital) and items with low margin but high turns (volume drivers worth protecting).
{"id": 24, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:item] WC2 inventory costing algorithms mined 2026-07-04. Key findings for Alice: (1) Average cost = (old_avg × old_qty + receipt_value + landed_costs) / new_qty — recalculated on every inbound. (2) FIFO walks cost layers oldest→newest, draining qty_available. (3) LIFO walks newest→oldest. (4) BUG FIX: WC2 missed cost accumulation on partial layer consumption — WC3 must add cost for partial lots. (5) Layers created on PO receipt, WO completion, positive adjustment. (6) Deficit layers auto-created at avg cost when stock goes negative. (7) Margin velocity = margin × turns. Alice should compute: for each item, (sale_price - cost_avg) / cost_avg × annual_turns. Flag items where margin_velocity drops below threshold. (8) PO layers start journal_complete=False — Alice should flag unreconciled PO layers older than 30 days.
{"id": 25, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE observe:item] WC3 inventory model review 2026-07-04. InventoryLayer already has: quantity JSON (received/issued/scrapped), remaining_qty(), mark_issue(), issue_or_enqueue() with reservation-awareness and pending queue when locked, cost JSON (unit_po, landed, freight, duty, handling, fifo/lifo snapshots, moving_avg, trend_pct), warehouse FK, lot tracking, serial_numbers list, source_doc linkage, is_locked flag. SiteInventory: aggregated rollup per item+site. Warehouse: name, code, site_code, location JSON, count JSON with aisle/column/shelf/bin granularity. Bill's insight: layer IS the count — split/move creates new layer records at original cost. JSON viewer = counting tool. Services needed: (1) create_layer on receipt, (2) consume_fifo/lifo, (3) split_layer to N locations, (4) transfer_layer between warehouses, (5) count_check (compare expected vs actual), (6) average_cost recalc, (7) margin_velocity per item.
{"id": 26, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:item] WC2 warehouse mining complete 2026-07-04. Alice training additions: (1) Location hierarchy is W-A-C-S-B (Warehouse-Aisle-Column-Shelf-Bin). (2) Multi-site activates when >1 site exists. (3) WC2 had no physical count module — WC3 will be better with QR scan → JSON viewer → count comparison. (4) Warehouse transfers = paired negative/positive movements with same transfer_id. (5) All inventory events flow through one pipeline: stage → apply → tally (async). (6) Weighted average cost recalculated at tally time, not event time. (7) Alice should watch for: unprocessed events (processed_at=null older than 1 hour), site buckets out of sync with layers, transfer pairs where one side completed but other didn't.
{"id": 27, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:item] Inventory services deployed 2026-07-04. Alice's nightly responsibilities: (1) Run update_item_margin_velocity() — stores margin_velocity, margin_pct, annual_turns, velocity_category in every item's metadata.health. (2) Run classify_abc() — categorize A/B/C for cycle count scheduling. (3) Run check_orphaned_events() — find unprocessed movements older than 1 hour, create Actions. (4) Watch for deficit alerts (negative inventory) — Action records created automatically. (5) Flag items where velocity_category changes (star→dead_capital is a signal). (6) Flag unreconciled PO layers (journal_complete=false) older than 30 days. Margin velocity categories: dead_capital (>50% margin, <2 turns), volume_driver (<10% margin, >50 turns), star (>20% margin, >20 turns).
{"id": 28, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:item] Margin velocity promoted to real Item fields 2026-07-04. Four new columns: margin_velocity (decimal, indexed), margin_pct (decimal), annual_turns (decimal), velocity_category (char, indexed). All sortable and filterable in DataBrowser — no JSON drilling needed. Alice runs update_item_margin_velocity() nightly to keep scores current. Tested on BB200 kit: 78% margin, 0 turns (no sales yet) = dead_capital. When sales start posting, turns will populate and velocity scores will become meaningful. Alice should watch for category transitions — an item going from star to dead_capital is a signal that sales dropped or cost increased.
{"id": 29, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE pattern:item] PO line cost cascade defined 2026-07-04. Three levels (most specific wins): (1) Setting: global default purchase behavior (use_last_cost, use_avg, use_vendor_price). (2) Vendor metadata: OrgItem or vendor.metadata overrides for this vendor's pricing. (3) Item metadata: item.cost.last or item-specific purchase cost override. Alice should validate: when a PO line cost is entered that differs significantly from the cascade default, flag it for review. Also: JSON viewer on PO lines should display avg cost, last cost, vendor price, and full layer stack for buyer context.
{"id": 30, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE pattern:item] XRef system mined 2026-07-04. The full purchasing chain: Item (BB005) → ItemXRef (vendor's part# WIL-A1010, with lead_time, sequence) → OrgItem (vendor's price $4.80) → PO line default cost. Alice should monitor: (1) Items with zero ItemXRef records (no vendor mapping — can't auto-fill PO lines). (2) OrgItem prices that diverge from ItemXRef costs (stale pricing). (3) ItemXRef.sequence for preferred vendor ordering. (4) Vendor catalog imports should create/update ItemXRef records. The relationship_type field enables product relationships beyond vendor mapping: substitute, accessory, replaces, alternate — Alice should track these for recommendation engines.
{"id": 31, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:item] Data identity rule from Bill 2026-07-04: UUID is the same for the same item from different sources. When Ingrid normalizes supplier catalogs, product identity resolution comes FIRST — one UUID per product, regardless of how many vendors supply it. Two vendors selling the same baseball = one Item (one UUID), two ItemXRef records (vendor part numbers), two OrgItem records (vendor prices). Never duplicate Items for the same product. Alice should watch for: duplicate Items that represent the same product from different vendors — these should be merged into one Item with multiple ItemXRef/OrgItem records. Match by UPC, manufacturer part#, or description similarity.
{"id": 32, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:item] Inventory reservation services rebuilt 2026-07-04. Replaces WC2 non-journalable invoice hack. Order allocation workflow: reserve_for_order (FIFO across layers, splits if needed) → commit_order_reservations (when invoice ships) → release_order_reservations (on cancel). Alice nightly: run release_expired() to reclaim stale holds. Alice should monitor: (1) items with high reserved-to-available ratio (demand signal), (2) reservations approaching expiry without commit (stalled orders), (3) partial allocations (not enough stock to fully reserve — backorder signal). Terminology: LAYER not stack — layers accumulate, erode, split. Stack is wrong metaphor.
{"id": 33, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:item] Pricing/catalog system mined from WC2 2026-07-04. Alice's pricing responsibilities: (1) Monitor catalog effectiveness — which promotions drove orders vs expired unused. (2) Flag below-margin prices — WC2 only had a console warning, WC3 needs real approval Actions. (3) Track price_locked lines — manual overrides that bypass the cascade. (4) Monitor PriceMatrix/qty-break usage — are volume buyers hitting their breaks? (5) Catalog expiration alerts — flag catalogs approaching dt_effective_end. (6) The pricing cascade is: factor→matrix→catalog_rule→price_level→customer_blanket→base_price. Alice should know which step resolved the price for any given line. Full resolution: price_resolver(item, contact, qty, date) returns (price, basis, discount_pct, catalog_id).
{"id": 34, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:item] Price resolver built 2026-07-04. Single function: resolve_price(item, contact, qty, date) returns PriceResolution with full audit trail. Cascade: locked→OrgItem→catalog_universal_pct→catalog_line→contact_price_level→base_price. First match wins, no stacking. Below margin_floor auto-creates Action for approval. Alice responsibilities: (1) Monitor resolved_by distribution — what % of prices come from each cascade step. (2) Flag below-margin approvals. (3) Track catalog effectiveness — which catalogs actually matched in pricing decisions. (4) Watch for items where base_price is the only resolver (no catalogs, no OrgItem — pricing not optimized). (5) Alert when catalogs approach expiration. Tested: BB200=$69.99 (78% margin), BB005=$2.50 (118%), BB103=$12.99 (500%).
{"id": 35, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:invoice] Sales dashboard service built 2026-07-04. CRITICAL RULE: below-margin prices NEVER block shipment. Flag and ship, review in dashboard. Dashboard: compute_dashboard(period_days, salesperson_id, rep_id, customer_id, margin_floor) returns transaction counts by type, margin velocity for period, above/below margin counts, star/dead_capital/volume_driver item lists. Switchable by date range, salesperson, rep, customer. Alice computes nightly, dashboard displays. This is Alice's primary reporting surface. The Action created for below-margin is informational (priority=low), not a gate.
{"id": 36, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:action] Commerce Dashboard design 2026-07-04: Single page with 4 tabs (Sales, Purchasing, Inventory, Velocity) sharing one filter bar. Small business users see everything in one place. Alice computes all four metric sets. The filter bar drives all tabs simultaneously — change the date range or vendor, everything updates. Tabs can be popped into separate windows on desktop via the spawn pattern. Alice training: quiz on what each tab shows, how filters work, what velocity categories mean (star/dead_capital/volume_driver).
{"id": 37, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:action] Alice coaching permissions defined 2026-07-04. Alice needs a permission level in her Setting record for WCHQ communication. Levels: 'none' (fully local), 'receive_only' (get coaching, don't share — DEFAULT), 'send_only' (share patterns, don't receive), 'full' (bidirectional). This is a sovereignty control — business owner decides. Also: Alice should creatively combine dashboard displays based on user navigation patterns. If she sees a user always filtering Sales by customer then switching to Velocity, she should suggest/create a combined preset view. The observe→pattern→recommend→promote loop applies to dashboard layouts too, not just data patterns.
{"id": 38, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:action] Alice WCHQ coaching framing 2026-07-04. Users must understand: only metadata is shared (navigation patterns, layout preferences, dashboard presets). NEVER business data (customers, prices, invoices, transactions). Same framing as Claude's 'can we use your conversation to improve.' UI presents four plain-language choices: Receive tips / Share patterns / Both / Neither. Default: Receive tips only. Alice quiz question: 'What data does Alice share with WCHQ when coaching is enabled?' Answer: 'Only navigation patterns and layout preferences — never business data, customers, prices, or transactions.'
{"id": 39, "status": "logged"}

[OBSERVE promote:item] Manufacturer sales reporting service concept 2026-07-04. Retailers opt in via Setting record to report product sales back to manufacturers through WCHQ/Ingrid. Three permission levels: 'none' (don't report), 'aggregate_only' (qty by region, no store identity), 'full' (store-level). Manufacturer never sees customer data — only product movement by location. Key value: manufacturer runs a Facebook ad in Tulsa, sees which retailers moved product, where inventory is low, whether marketing spend worked. This closes the loop: Ingrid pushes catalog data DOWN to retailers (product info, pricing), retailers push sales data UP to manufacturers (movement, inventory levels). Same UUID identifies the product everywhere. Alice should track: which manufacturers are requesting this data, which retailers have opted in, aggregate reporting compliance.

C) Search by date D) Check each serial manually
Correct: B — warranty_due queries issued serials where warranty.dt_end is within the window

C) Only History D) Only Customer
Correct: B — SpawnLinks component shows all 6 related-window buttons for serials

Correct: A — OrgItem is step 2 in the cascade, catalog is step 3. First match wins, no stacking.

Correct: B — two levels, most specific wins

Correct: B — Wisdom of the Many, sovereignty preserved

Q2 (intermediate): A rep loads a saved search. Can they see all customers?
A) Yes B) No — user's query_scope from field_access Setting is ALWAYS injected. Reps only see their assigned customers.
Correct: B — security is mandatory, not optional

Q3 (intermediate): A user filters orders by status=planned 7 times this week. What does Alice do?
A) Nothing B) Batch coaching: "You've filtered orders by status=planned 7 times. Want me to save this as a quick search?" Stores in Contact.metadata.saved_searches.
Correct: B — observe, suggest, don't force

Q4 (advanced): Can power users see the SQL?
A) Direct SQL exposed B) No — the config JSON is the query in human-readable form ({model, filters, sort}). Opens in JSON viewer. Advanced users edit the JSON. Translated to Django ORM server-side.
Correct: B — config IS the query, never raw SQL

[OBSERVE promote:item] Forecasting Training 2026-07-04.

Q1: What is the demand forecast formula?
A) Just orders B) Proposal lines × probability + order lines = expected demand. A $100K proposal at 60% = $60K expected.
Correct: B — probability-weighted pipeline

Q2: What makes the cash flow forecast a "swag"?
A) It's precise B) It estimates: due_date + net_days_delay = when cash arrives. Proposals weighted by probability. Inventory carrying cost estimated from on_hand × cost × carrying_days. Good enough for planning, not accounting.
Correct: B

Q3: Does forecasting need a new model?
A) Yes B) No — computed view across existing transaction lines (proposals, orders, POs, WOs, inventory layers, ledger). No new table.
Correct: B

Alice should monitor: items where demand exceeds supply (purchasing signal), items where cash-out exceeds cash-in (liquidity signal), proposals sitting at high probability without converting to orders (stalled pipeline).

[OBSERVE pattern:system] Pricing insight: JPods sells CERTAINTY, not just speed. Mesh redundancy guarantees arrival time. A 15-min trip that is always 15 min is worth more than a 10-min trip that is sometimes 45 min. Alice can charge a premium for guaranteed arrival because mesh network makes it credible. Fare reflects reliability, not just distance. We cannot buy time at any price — when a customer is stuck, no refund makes them whole.
